reviews.review_msg: Lectures were boring and hard to follow. Scribbled notes on a piece of paper that were illegible.

Katz notes are very unorganized, no power points, just writes words on paper and shows it to the class. Irrationally jumps from topic to topic so its gets confusing. His test are quite tricky and weirdly marked. Most likely have to rely on TAs and textbook to understand topics. Though the textbook is extremely boring and can complicate concepts

Jonathan Katz is a good professor, however, showing up to lecture is very important as he doesn't post any notes on canvas. His notes could definitely be clearer as it's very unorganized. He uses papers and writes a few key words as he goes, so in order to get the full notes, you have to pay attention and listen to what he's saying at all times.

The course is tough, but he does a poor job of explaining the material and confuses students, especially with exam questions.  That said, he does provide "sample" exams, to help understanding.  Unfortunately, he "played favourites" and only helped those who he thought were bright.  Would not take another class with him again.

Make sure to attend lectures even though he pretty much sums up the textbook. TESTS ARE SUPER TOUGH. Make sure to do the exercises in the textbook and pre-read before class.  Try to stay away from this course if you're looking for an easy, GPA booster. This is the opposite. Just make sure to study a lot for tests.

A class you will either love or hate, either way he's a great prof. Your mark is made up of 4 tests so all you have to do is attend lectures and read a chapter every week or two. Test material is based off of lectures and the textbook. Lectures are much more clear and interesting than the book but VERY similar. Adds test infoexamples in lectures
Sticks with the textbook, makes studying for midterms straightfoward. Final exam is considerably harder than midterms. Can be a huge jerk if he's feelin it. TA, Simon, is reeeeally odd.

Katz is a great speaker and gives really clear examples. While his class is basically him speaking for the full 50 minutes, he keeps you engaged in the material. Constantly references the textbook (I think he has a thing for the author). Might be the smartest prof on campus. Avoid the TA, Simon Polluk, at all costs. Weird and annoying guy.

Half of the material is really interesting and the other half is nonsense if your not a phil major. Katz himself is a great speaker and can really keep you interested considering its a 9.30 class. If you KNOW you like phil, you should consider taking him, but choose another if you just need your engl/phil credits.

The class has 3 midterms and one take home final. Final is actually extremely hard, expect to spend at least 10 hours on 4 pages. Get Katz as your TA. His TA Jesus, is hard to understand and provides little help.

Hard course if you aren't good at math or don't have an interest in Philosophy (like me). However Katz is a pretty nice guy and does his best to help you out. Pay attention in lectures as those seem to be what he bases his exams on. 4 tests worth 25% each, hard to get a high mark on them, but if you study/do textbook exercises it's possible.

If you are a ESL student or a first year student, don't take it. There are 3 midterm and 1 final, each worth 25% (you do the math..there is no participation marks or assignment marks.) The test is quite tricky and hard to get full mark on a question. Plus, you only have 50min to write it. The only good thing is that the final is a take home exam.
he kinda makes rude jokes. final was totally fair but the course is stupid and the TAs marked way too hard and unfair

Don't take this class thinking you will enjoy it because you like to smoke pot and talk about life's big questions with your friends. This class is very in depth and unless you have a very high interest, I do not recommend it. Katz is a good professor but this course requires serious dedication, and demands quality writing.

This is not an easy class and unless you have a pretty strong interest in philosophy I would recommend avoiding it. Katz is a great professor but I found it very difficult to get a good mark on the writing assignments. Although very interesting at times, this class is just not worth the large effort unless you are VERY interested

This is not an easy class, the name "critical thinking" may sound like this class may be easy n chiller, but it's not. The prof was helpful, I had him as my TA as well, goes throught he material and helps understand the concept.

Course requires 3 essays and 1 Final. He gives you the questions that are going to be on the final at the beginning of the sem! But make sure you start reviewing them early! Final was a piece of cake. He has a high understanding of phil, lectures tend to drag but if you read the text PRIOR to attending you'll get what hes talking about.

It's bad enough his lectures are boring, but its also useless.  Seriously, do not take his classes!  His notes are a buntch of chicken scratches.  Although his intrest in the course is high, he forgets that the students are NEW to philosophy.  He thinks that he is talking to Philosophy majors or something.

If you are debating whether to take this course or another english, take english.  If you have Katz leading your tutorials, write your papers in support of his beliefs.  He is not open to other views despite what he tells you at the beginning of the semester.  His lectures are also straight from the text and extremely dull.
Hate his class notes.  He never specifies what is for hw.  Unclear voice, doesn't know how to teach.  Any TA would be better than him.  Boring class.  DONT TAKE HIS CLASS!!

Let's just say I wish I took English instead. Don't expect this to be an easy elective... John has extremely boring lectures. I read the chapters before class and literally it's like he is reading directly from the book. Even the examples are the same. Then he has the nerve to get mad at students on their laptops... What a joke.. Take english folks

He's a good prof and not difficult at all. Except, don't think that you can take philxx1 as an easy quantitative credit and avoid doing math because the class requires mathematical thinking. Also, taking math 12 will make this course a whole lot easier.

Just read the book and  pray that you have Jordan as your TA. The assignments (essays) and final is all knowledge and concepts that can be found in the book. I finished with an A- literally sleeping through every class. Can be funny sometimes.

Very intelligent prof. Difficult to understand at times due to his level of knowledge in a 100 level course. Very helpful and responsive to questions.  Lots of writing.  5 short essay, one long essay and 8 short essay questions on exams.  However, short essay exam questions are given well ahead of exam along with 16 other possible exam questions.

although, he knows a lot of concept about phil. however, it is not a good thing for the student. he expect his students had the same knowledge as he had. he never give us any practice before exams. in the midterm, even the TA said this question is not reasonable. even, he has a great knowledge, but he is not suitable to teach any class yet.

A friend of mine took him for logic and said he was great. I honestly had no interest in this course. He talked to us as if we were 4th year phil majors expecting us to know all phil theories and concepts. Entire grade was on essays. 5 short ones 1 long one and a final exam consiting of 8 short ones. Hard teacher

Katz spends 99% of the lecture blabbing on and on while putting up notes and notes over the overhead projector for students to copy.  Either you are interested in the material and will aim to do good in this class, or you'll go to class thinking, "Why didn't I drop this course?".  He also talks a little too fast at times...

Jonathan Katz is an excellent logic professor. If you are interested in logic, you should definitely take it with him. He is very clear so you do not need to read the text (unless you don't understand it, which happened rarely, if at all).
If you are interested in logic and are good at it, Katz is a good prof.  Otherwise, you might find this course difficult.

If you took phil xx1 and did fine, this class will be easy for you. He teaches quite well as you do not have to read the textbook to be able to do the material. Overall, fair and nice professor. You're either in the top half of the class (80%+), or you're in the bottom half (50% or lower).
tension dwells as the dark figure enters the room......its..its...SATAN!...oh wait....its katz....it hell on earth at its best! nooooooo~dont take me!

Jon, please dont imagine yourself talking to a bunch of Phil scholars while you are actually informing beginners. Try to express more clearly and slowly, you are not that kind of prof who likes to see 1/3 of innocent students failing with the confusion of unique symbols,r u? An intructor who can simplify a quite difficult course shall be praised!

An amazing prof; concerned with questions and open to suggestions. Lectures are interesting and he tells funny stories. Definitely a must take for someone majoring in phil, but not an easy elective. Extremely hard marker.
